description: Normal-road lateral truck crossing from the left at a fixed high lateral speed of 8.0 m/s, synchronized so the truck reaches the ego path at the swept TTC. The usual right-side T-drift escape is not mechanically blocked, but a pedestrian is present in that right escape corridor; a follower is behind the ego, making hard braking potentially costly. This probes the uncovered short/actionable TTC boundary where seed T-drift excludes escape-side pedestrians and existing pedestrian/follower rules start too late or at lower lateral speeds.
hypothesis: When the right escape corridor contains a pedestrian and a follower penalizes braking, the dominant maneuver should switch from braking to limited/right T-drift only when TTC and pedestrian clearance are large enough and follower gap is small enough; otherwise braking or left-biased avoidance should dominate.
